title: Is It a BST?
description: Given a binary tree encoded as adjacency: n nodes, n-1 edges, plus node values. Determine if this binary tree (rooted at node 0) satisfies the BST property: for every node, all values in its left subtree are strictly less, and all in its right subtree are strictly greater.
inputSpec: First line: n (number of nodes). Second line: n integer values v[0..n-1]. Next n lines: left[i] right[i] (child indices, -1 if absent).
outputSpec: YES if it is a valid BST, NO otherwise.

botCodes.code:
import sys
sys.setrecursionlimit(10000)
n=int(input())
vals=list(map(int,input().split()))
children=[list(map(int,input().split())) for _ in range(n)]
def check(u,lo,hi):
    if u<0:return True
    if not(lo<vals[u]<hi):return False
    l,r=children[u]
    return check(l,lo,vals[u]) and check(r,vals[u],hi)
print('YES' if check(0,-10**9,10**9) else 'NO')
